data.text: a usually flexible container that may be closed for holding, storing, or carrying something: such as
examples: a bag of tricks
a bag of tricks
a bag of tricks
The tent bagged outward under the force of the wind.
pants bagging in the back
bag groceries
arrived early and bagged a front row seat
… he was bagged by police who were alerted by the freelance jewel salesman.
bag a playoff berth
decided to bag her job and move to the country
Tiger's been losing his temper and his putting stroke. He shed his agent, and he bagged his caddy …
I asked the respiratory therapist to continue bagging and assisting the patient while I sought the family's permission not to proceed with intubation.

data.text: a woman with no established residence who roams the streets of a city carrying her possessions in shopping bags
a lunch prepared at home and packed inside a bag or box to be eaten elsewhere —called also box lunch
examples: His circle of students grew, and each Tuesday they met to eat bag lunches and talk.

data.text: the double-walled fluid-filled sac that encloses and protects the fetus in the womb and that breaks releasing its fluid during the birth process
a waterproof bag into which a person (such as an airplane passenger) who is experiencing nausea can vomit
examples: Nonetheless, he has his face in the barf bag on this ridiculously cramped and bumpy twin-propeller flight from Palm Springs to Los Angeles …

data.text: a fabric bag for carrying personal equipment; especially: duffel bag
